iterate_target: Leaderboard walk-forward champion on ETH/4h: supertrend_tail_harvester_8to1 (QScore 4.47, PF 1.921, DD 10.9, 85 trades)
thesis_hint: Iterate on a passing strategy this cycle. Live context shows recent completed work is still explore-heavy overall, but the recent scored results also show a strong concentration in failing 1h batches and repeated ETH/1h channel-shortbias pass-neighbors. To preserve the soft 70/30 doctrine without over-farming the same 1h family, rotate the exploit turn into the supported ETH/4h walk-forward champion from the leaderboard. Demand 2-3 focused variants only: one should reduce drawdown from the 10.9% baseline without materially collapsing the 85-trade density, one should improve continuation quality by requiring slightly cleaner trend-state context before entry, and an optional third may tighten post-entry invalidation only if PF stays near the champion profile. Do not convert this into ETH/1h channel or KAMA-stoch logic, do not broaden to unsupported transfers, and do not waste the batch on generic threshold spray.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one variant improves the current pass with walk-forward QScore >= 4.60, profit_factor >= 1.95, total_trades >= 75, and max_drawdown_pct <= 10.0, or (B) 3 focused variants are exhausted without beating the current ETH/4h champion baseline. Abort any variant with total_trades < 60, profit_factor < 1.40, or max_drawdown_pct > 13.0.
rotation_reason: Allow exploitation this cycle: recent completed work is still explore-heavy, 1h is the more concentrated recent completed lane, and ETH/4h tail harvester is a supported walk-forward champion that rotates away from the repeated ETH/1h pass cluster while staying inside the proven passing set.
